extra.reasoning:
The property requires that toggling leg_swelling from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. It is VIOLATED.

leg_swelling appears only in r5 (priority 100), which fires only when all ten of hemoptysis, stiff_neck, focal_weakness, pallor, severe_headache, rash, abdominal_pain, vomiting, chest_pain, leg_swelling are true, and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). Because r5 has priority 100 — far above the priority-3 emergent rules — activating it overrides everything.

Witness baseline (leg_swelling = false), with the other nine r5-conjuncts all true (back_pain irrelevant, set false):
- r5 does not fire (needs leg_swelling). Firing rules at the top priority (3) are r4 (vomiting), r6 (pallor), r7 (stiff_neck), r11 (rash), all setting emergent. Highest-priority winner => acuity = emergent (highest level).

Flip leg_swelling to true (everything else unchanged):
- Now all ten conjuncts of r5 hold, so r5 fires with priority 100, beating the priority-3 emergent rules. Winner => acuity = self_care (lowest level).

Thus acuity drops from emergent to self_care when leg_swelling is set true. enum order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent confirms self_care is strictly lower than emergent. The monotonicity property is violated. The counterexample assignment given is the BEFORE state (leg_swelling=false); setting its leg_swelling to true produces the lowered acuity.
